behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff (parser success must flow into normal hook input handling), not just a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text says only payloads parse and proceed through handling, with no real process/OS/network requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process parser + hook input handler. Feed valid JSON whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and $vars. Assert parse succeeds, handler takes non-error path, and no parse-failure trace is emitted.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is parser error propagation into trace-record construction (cross-module). REAL-INFRA: not required; malformed bytes can be injected in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires trace record content, not subprocess/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process parser + trace sink. Submit malformed JSON variants (unterminated string, bad comma, broken brace). Assert `json_parse_failed` is recorded and context fields identify location/snippet sufficient for diagnosis.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is policy mapping across parser error object -> handled result + telemetry intent, which is module interaction. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior explicitly says local error-policy path.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger parse failure through parser module and pass resulting error into real error-policy component. Assert returned result is handled (no exception escapes) and telemetry-intent flag/event is set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit requirement for real process execution and malformed stdin behavior (subprocess/OS pipe + exit behavior). MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss crash/exit-order issues. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because text explicitly requires real process execution.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn actual hook process, write malformed payload to stdin, close pipe, and wait for exit. Assert non-crash termination behavior per policy and that expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior names real invocation path starting at stdin; needs actual process I/O path to catch failures at OS/process boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/runner can hide framing/flush/order defects. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due to explicit 'real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute hook through real entrypoint with malformed stdin. Assert resulting trace artifact from configured sink matches expected failure signal/schema for parse error.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is classification of outcomes across two pipeline paths (valid markdown-in-JSON vs malformed JSON), requiring parser+error handling+trace wiring together. REAL-INFRA: not required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because no real subprocess/OS requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run parameterized in-process cases through parser/handling pipeline: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Assert only malformed cases emit parse-failure signal; valid-markdown cases follow success path without misattribution.
